item.dc:creator: notthisidagain

item.title: I met Mister Savage at FanExpo in Denver CO
item.description:
He got down on one knee. Most people just hover over me.
Best day ever! Update! I made viral!!! I don't know if he will see this but I appreciate his team or the staff at FanExpo helping him. I recently had back surgery on July 3rd and found out last minute he was attending, I didn't even know FanExpo was this weekend. It's been one of my dreams to meet him and I am so happy I did. Back to recovery.
item.dc:creator: allthefoodIputinmymouth

item.title: Landing a B29
item.description: I used to want to fly one but….having seen the huge control inputs at “slow” speed that have minimal effect, I no longer desire that. Makes you appreciate the men that had to take one of these into battle with a massive bomb load on board! That extra weight would give you even less control!
item.dc:creator: mercbrit

item.title: No question about that ....
item.description: If you've ever wondered what the word of a Supreme Court Justice is worth .... "I believe that no one is above the law under our system and that includes the president." ~ now-Chief Justice John Roberts in his 2005 confirmation hearing "No person in this country, no matter how high or powerful, is above the law." ~ Justice Samuel Alito, in his 2006 confirmation hearing "We're all equal before the law...The foundation of our Constitution was that... the presidency would not be a monarchy...[T]he president is not above the law, no one is above the law." ~ Justice Brett Kavanaugh to the Senate in 2018
item.dc:creator: blank666man

item.title: A Lot of Dogs, Probably; a Forty-seventh Dump
item.description:
I believe in hims.
Way ahead of you, babes.
I want to take Iggy swimming but outside hurts.
I am like 3 pages away from finally finishing my GOT colouring book. It's been years.
And it'll be something stupid like Noodle-Steak The Ambivalent.
Don't get too excited, I'm really boring and never leave my apartment.
I don't even like cinnamon.
No but man ... If I was a rich girl. Nanananana.
I just crushed a medium meat lover's by myself. It was glorious except it cost $32. I have regrets.
Iggy is frequently double-flipped.
My budget for Iggy is insane. Thank goodness for insurance.
You never have to ask.
I can't believe someone else does this. I am not comforted.
When I take Iggy out at night, the mosquitoes are SO BAD I have to wear a hoodie and jeans. Is there really an E in mosquitoes? Are you SURE, GOOGLE?!
Noodles drained, ditch the seasoning packet. Rice vinegar, soy sauce, sriracha, minced garlic, sesame oil, egg. Delightful.
Oh Jorlgrf.
Iggy tax. Baby girl needs the AC after her walks. Poor lil brachycephalic booger.
item.dc:creator: AshesVonDust

item.title: Another sewing project: Dominique dress by Payterncos
item.description:
Recently-ish I made a waistcoat from a pattern by a designer called Patterncos on Etsy. I loved the pattern and wanted to try more of her designs. This is what happened. Disclaimer that I am somewhat of a beginner at garment sewing, so it might not be the beeeest representation of this dress design. I think I overestimated my skill level going into this pattern. Or underestimated the difficulty level. Or who knows maybe both. But it was fun, I was humbled, and I learned some new things.
I was looking for fabric that-like the waistcoat's fabric-was shiny and moved with the light. It's just so pretty and I thought it would be so fun for this dress! I found this on Etsy, a silk jaquard, and I used an interfacing as well. No lining, as per the the pattern instructions.
First a mock up with some scrap cotton. Which was a great idea because it took 4 tries and alterations to the pattern before I finally started with my actual shiny fabric.
The main cause of the issues was fit and this stupid zipper bulge. I asked on Reddit what was possibly causing the bulge, and the consensus was too small/too low waist. So some of the mock ups were adjusting the size and height of the waist. It was greet advice and it helped. But the main cause of the zipper bulge turns out was me being arrogant and thinking I could just wing it because HoW hArD caN iT bE tO pUt IN aZiPPeR? Well it's hard if you don't use interfacing and do it a certain way but luckily I ate my pride and found some lovely people on youtube that explained it well. So I made my last mockup with proper zipper technique, and it all looked good
Newly as and properly installed invisible zipper in the final piece. Did that part first.
This pattern was a bit tricky, partly because the curved seam lines. It is a not super pronounced princess seams in some parts.
I spilled my spaghetti :( and stepped on some that fell on the floor :(
Also lost bobbin chicken more times than I'd like to admit.
Quick check on the fit before adding straps and finishing off. Lest the thing bulges again or doesn't fit despite the mock up fitting.
I'm also just super proud of how invisible the invisible zipper is :) in the future, I might try lining up the pattern pieces better.
Straps on, dress finished and hemmed and then I noticed it was bulging again, and not in a way I saw with the mockup. Interfaced jaquard behaves and drapes differently than a single layer of cotton muslin does. I was about to flip, maybe even temporarily give up, but luckily with some pinning and holding and stuff I saw I could make some vertical darts in the back and hide them in the seams. So I tried that first and it worked!
Love his face :)
All done! Overall I'd say pattern and instructions were good, I think I could have benefited with more practice before tackling this one. This pattern was trickier than other ones I had done because of the princess-like seams, and also all the adjustments I needed to make to the pattern to get it to fit. The pattern is made up of many pieces which means it's super customizable...but also means that for every one change you make to a piece you may need to make changes to multiple other pieces too. I'd say this one is not great for beginners, but advanced intermediates would probably find it easy enough? I learned a lot from this project. I will be making another one of these dresses. I found a red fitted black fabric that made me think of Sisters of Mercy's first and last and always album, and with the way the center front and back are these long panels...well if I make the center front panels out of solid black it would look like the album and I think that's awesome.
Finished dress, flat.
item.dc:creator: TheStitchingSquid
